title: Lab 11: Time Synchronization
description: Configure server as a simple chrony source and point client at it.

tasks: On server, configure chronyd so it serves time to the 192.168.122.0/24 lab network and starts automatically at boot.
On client, configure chronyd so it synchronizes only with server and starts automatically at boot.
hints: server needs an allow rule before it can serve the lab subnet.
client should use a single server line for this lab.
checks: systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && grep -Rqs '^server server iburst$' /etc/chrony.d /etc/chrony.conf
# server systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && grep -Rqs '^allow 192.168.122.0/24$' /etc/chrony.d /etc/chrony.conf
solution_outline: Update chrony on server with an allow rule and enable the service.
Point client to server only and enable the service there too.
task_titles: Configure server as the chrony source
Configure client to use only server for time
